parameters.content:
## Meeting Action Items to ClickUp — Fireflies + GPT-4o-mini + Telegram

For founders, project managers, and team leads who want every meeting action item to automatically appear as a ClickUp task — without manual note-taking or copy-pasting. When Fireflies finishes transcribing a meeting, this workflow extracts action items from two sources: the Fireflies NLP task detector and the AI-generated summary. GPT-4o-mini parses each item into a clean task with an owner, deadline, and priority. Every task is created in ClickUp automatically. A single Telegram message confirms the total count when all tasks are done.

## How it works
- **1. Webhook — Fireflies Transcript Done** receives the meetingId from Fireflies on transcription complete
- **2. Code — Extract Meeting ID** safely extracts the meetingId from all possible Fireflies payload structures
- **3. IF — Valid Meeting ID?** validates the meetingId — invalid webhooks exit cleanly
- **5. Set — Config Values** stores Fireflies API key, ClickUp API token, ClickUp list ID, Telegram chat ID, and author details
- **6. HTTP — Fetch Transcript** calls the Fireflies GraphQL API for sentences with ai_filters.task and summary.action_items
- **7. Code — Process Action Items** combines both sources, deduplicates, and limits to 15 items
- **8. IF — Action Items Found?** stops cleanly if no action items were detected
- **10. AI Agent — Parse Action Items** uses GPT-4o-mini to parse each item into taskName, owner, deadline, and priority
- **13. Code — Split Tasks for Loop** converts the array to one item per task and sets isLastTask on the final one
- **14. HTTP — Create ClickUp Task** creates each task in ClickUp via API v2 with priority and due date
- **15. IF — All Tasks Created?** checks the isLastTask flag — only the last task triggers Telegram
- **16. Code — Build Telegram Summary** builds the notification message with meeting title and total task count
- **17. Telegram — Send Task Summary** sends one confirmation message after all tasks are created

## Set up steps
1. Activate the workflow and copy the webhook URL from node 1
2. In Fireflies — go to Settings, Developer Settings, Webhooks, and paste the URL
3. In **5. Set — Config Values** — replace YOUR_FIREFLIES_API_KEY, YOUR_CLICKUP_API_TOKEN, YOUR_CLICKUP_LIST_ID, YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, your name, and company name
4. In **11. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
5. In **17. Telegram — Send Task Summary** — connect your Telegram Bot API credential and send /start to your bot before testing

## Webhook Receipt and Validation
Fireflies POSTs a meetingId here on transcription complete. A Code node safely extracts the meetingId from any payload structure. Invalid webhooks exit cleanly via the Set node below.

nodes.name: Section — Config, Transcript Fetch, and Action Item Extraction

parameters.content:
## Config, Transcript Fetch, and Action Item Extraction
Config stores all credentials and author details. Fireflies GraphQL API fetches the transcript. A Code node extracts action items from two sources: NLP task-flagged sentences and the AI summary list. Combined, deduplicated, limited to 15.

## AI Task Parsing
GPT-4o-mini (temperature 0.2) parses each raw action item into four structured fields: a clean verb-first task name, the responsible owner, a deadline in YYYY-MM-DD format, and a priority from 1 to 4. Structured Output Parser enforces the array schema.

## ClickUp Task Creation Loop
Converts the tasks array to one item per task. Creates each task in ClickUp via API v2 with name, description, priority, and due date. The isLastTask flag ensures Telegram fires exactly once after all tasks are created.

## Telegram Notification
Builds and sends a single confirmation message after all ClickUp tasks are created. Includes meeting title, date, and total task count.

assignments.name: result
assignments.value: =No action items found or transcript not ready for: {{ $json.meetingId }}

parameters.text:
=You are a project manager parsing meeting action items into structured ClickUp tasks.

MEETING: {{ $json.meetingTitle }}
DATE: {{ $json.meetingDate }}
PARTICIPANTS: {{ $json.participants }}
OVERVIEW: {{ $json.overview }}

ACTION ITEMS FROM MEETING:
{{ $json.actionItemsRaw }}

For EACH action item above, extract the following and return a JSON array.

Return ONLY a valid JSON array. No extra text. No markdown. No backticks.
Each element in the array must have exactly these 4 fields:

taskName — A clean, actionable task title. Start with a verb. Maximum 60 characters. Example: Send project proposal to client by Friday

owner — The person responsible. Extract from the action item text if mentioned. If not mentioned, write: Unassigned

deadline — The deadline in YYYY-MM-DD format. Extract from the action item if a date or timeframe is mentioned. Examples: today = {{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}, tomorrow = {{ $now.plus({days: 1}).toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}, this week = {{ $now.plus({days: 3}).toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}, next week = {{ $now.plus({days: 7}).toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}. If no deadline mentioned, default to {{ $now.plus({days: 2}).toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}

priority — A number: 1 for urgent, 2 for high, 3 for normal, 4 for low. Use context to decide. If unclear use 3.

EXAMPLE OUTPUT FORMAT:
[{"taskName": "Send updated proposal to John by Friday", "owner": "Rahul", "deadline": "2026-04-12", "priority": 2}, {"taskName": "Schedule follow-up call with client", "owner": "Unassigned", "deadline": "2026-04-14", "priority": 3}]

RULES:
- Return ONLY the JSON array. Nothing else.
- Every item in the input must become exactly one task in the output.
- Do not skip any action items.
- Do not add tasks that were not in the input.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "name": "{{ $json.taskName }}",
  "description": "Meeting: {{ $json.meetingTitle }}\nDate: {{ $json.meetingDate }}\nOwner: {{ $json.owner }}\nDeadline: {{ $json.deadline }}\n\nAuto-created from meeting notes by n8n + Fireflies + GPT-4o-mini",
  "priority": {{ $json.priority }},
  "due_date": {{ $json.deadlineMs }},
  "due_date_time": true,
  "notify_all": false,
  "tags": ["meeting-action-item"]
}

assignments.name: result
assignments.value: =Task created. Continuing loop — not last task yet.
